author: song20
content:
Good day fellow Ethereum core researchers, 

I am trying to participate to EPF7 part-time, since I am already a full-time Master student in CS/Network Security.
I've read call for proposals by mentors (https://github.com/eth-protocol-fellows/cohort-seven/blob/main/projects/project-ideas.md),

and here's some topics I am interested in:
    ●  Exploring Incentive structure change from PBS to ePBS (in upcoming Glamsterdam), how each participants are affected and measure its actual impact, specifically in centralization perspective
       (I'm trying to contact to Robust Incentives Group (RIG) to coordinate this through email)
    ● Achieving stateless consensus - State Data Analysis
       Previously, I found that there are lots of out-versioned and **duplicated** smart contracts on chain. Some reseach highlishts this on DeFi ecosystem [1] (ex. ERC-20, DEX, ...). For quick check, you can find new Uniswap pool is created every 5 minutes [2]. Bad part is that over 50 (%) of the tokens are not used a day after creation [3]. I believe the labeling contracts should reflect this reality. Also, I believe Data analysis is one of my strongest capability I can contribute here.
    ● Other research-based topics (except for post-quantum research and technical implementation)

I'm thinking of teaming up (or joining a team if it exists) with few people
It'd be nice if there's anyone who shares same interest as I am, and work together!

Thanks!

[1] Pierro, Giuseppe Antonio, and Roberto Tonelli. "Analysis of source code duplication in ethreum smart contracts." 2021 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering (SANER). IEEE, 2021.
[2] https://etherscan.io/address/0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9cc5aA6f#internaltx
[3] Zhou, Yuanhang, et al. "Stop pulling my rug: Exposing rug pull risks in crypto token to investors." Proceedings of the 46th International Conference on Software Engineering: Software Engineering in Practice. 2024.
